steps.title: Join independent clauses
steps.content: The sentence links two independent clauses: compiling is not exclusive to certain programming languages, and any language can incorporate a compiler. The word "however" can work as a parenthetical contrast at the end of the first clause.

steps.title: Punctuate however cleanly
steps.content: A and C put "however" before the second clause but omit the comma it needs. B uses only commas between independent clauses, creating a comma splice. A comma before "however" and a semicolon after it correctly joins the clauses. The answer is D.
